etymology.description: 激(激) (jī, "stimulate") + 动(動) (dòng, "move") = to be stirred or excited in feelings
etymology.interpretation: The word 激(激) (jī, "stimulate") suggests applying energy or stimulation to something. 动(動) (dòng, "move") conveys the idea of movement or agitation. Together, 激动(激動) (jīdòng) indicates a state where the emotions have been stirred into a lively and excited condition, akin to how external forces can cause motion.

examples.chinese: 比赛胜利后(比賽勝利後)，他非常激动(他非常激動)。
examples.pinyin: Bǐsài shènglì hòu, tā fēicháng jīdòng.
examples.english: After the victory of the match, he was very excited.

examples.chinese: 听到这个消息，她激动得流泪(聽到這個消息，她激動得流淚)。
examples.pinyin: Tīngdào zhège xiāoxi, tā jīdòng dé liúlèi.
examples.english: Upon hearing the news, she was so excited that she cried.

examples.chinese: 孩子们对即将到来的旅行激动不已(孩子們對即將到來的旅行激動不已)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen duì jíjiāng dào lái de lǚxíng jīdòng bùyǐ.
examples.english: The children were endlessly excited for the upcoming trip.
